data.title: Oral Empirical Therapy of Fever in Low-Risk Neutropenic Cancer Patients: A Prospective, Double-Blind, Randomized, Multicenter Trial Comparing Monotherapy (Single Daily Dose Moxifloxacin) With Combination Therapy (Ciprofloxacin Plus Amoxicillin/Clavulanic Acid)
data.country: Turkey (Türkiye), Slovakia, Italy, Israel, Belgium, Germany, France, Switzerland
data.brief_summary:
RATIONALE: Antibiotics such as amoxicillin, ciprofloxacin, and moxifloxacin may be effective in preventing or controlling fever and neutropenia in patients with cancer. It is not yet known whether moxifloxacin alone is more effective than amoxicillin combined with ciprofloxacin in treating neutropenia and fever.

PURPOSE: This randomized clinical trial is studying how well moxifloxacin works and compares it to ciprofloxacin together with amoxicillin in treating neutropenia and fever in patients
data.eligibility_criteria:
DISEASE CHARACTERISTICS:

* Diagnosis of cancer with developing febrile neutropenia

  * Neutropenia defined as an absolute granulocyte count of less than 1,000/mm\^3, expected to fall to less than 500/mm\^3 within 24 hours, secondary to administration of chemotherapy and/or radiotherapy within the
endpoints: Response as measured by International Antimicrobial Therapy Group (IATG) specific criteria at the completion of allocated treatment
data.sponsor: European Organisation for Research and Treatment of Cancer - EORTC
